question: What is CppIndia?
answer: CppIndia is India's C++ user group, founded in 2020. We run bi-monthly tech talks, an annual conference (CppIndiaCon), and maintain a welcoming Discord community for C++ developers at all levels.

question: Who can join CppIndia?
answer: Anyone interested in C++ is welcome — from students writing their first program to senior engineers with decades of experience. We're inclusive, beginner-friendly, and free to join.

question: How do I join the community?
answer: The quickest way is to join our Discord server at discord.gg/Wz42tX5. You can also follow us on LinkedIn, YouTube, and Twitter to stay updated on talks and events.

question: Why learn C++ in 2026?
answer: C++ remains the language of choice for performance-critical systems — game engines, operating systems, embedded firmware, financial trading, compilers, and more. Modern C++ (C++17/20/23) is significantly safer and more expressive than older dialects. C++26 brings reflection, contracts, and further safety improvements.

question: Should I learn C++ or Rust?
answer: Both are excellent systems languages. C++ has a 40-year ecosystem, billions of lines of deployed code, and unmatched library support. Rust offers stronger memory safety guarantees by default. If you're starting fresh on a new systems project, both are valid choices. If you're working with existing C++ codebases, C++ is the pragmatic choice. Many developers learn both.

question: I'm a beginner. Where do I start with C++?
answer: Check our Learning Roadmap page for a curated path. Short answer: start with a good book (A Tour of C++ by Bjarne Stroustrup is excellent), set up a modern toolchain (Clang or GCC + CMake), and join our Discord where beginners get patient, friendly help.

question: Can I give a talk at CppIndia?
answer: Absolutely! We actively encourage first-time speakers. You don't need to be an expert — a 20-minute talk on something you learned recently is valuable. Visit our Call for Speakers page to submit a proposal.

question: What's the format for tech talks?
answer: Monthly talks are typically 30–45 minutes online via Discord/YouTube Live, followed by a Q&A. CppIndiaCon sessions are 20–45 minutes. All talks are recorded and published on our YouTube channel for free.

question: How can my company sponsor CppIndia?
answer: We offer Gold, Silver, and Bronze sponsorship tiers with different visibility and benefits. Sponsors get logo placement on the website, mentions in newsletters, and featured job listings. Contact us at info@cppindia.co.in or visit our Sponsors page.

question: Is everything free?
answer: Yes! All tech talks, conference recordings, resources, and community access are completely free. We're a volunteer-run community funded by our generous sponsors.
